document:
What is DIGIT Studio?

DIGIT Studio is the no-code way to build and scale public service delivery apps on DIGIT, by eGov Foundation.

The four stages of the DIGIT Studio lifecycle:
- Create: Transform ideas into digital services quickly with a no-code visual canvas or pre-created templates
- Validate: Ensure every service is aligned with users and stakeholders, compliant, and launch-ready
- Improve: Modify, evolve and optimize services through frictionless iteration
- Launch: Add data, create users and go live quickly

DIGIT Studio enables governments to Create, Validate, Iterate and Launch Digital Apps for Public Service Delivery.

Why DIGIT Studio? The problem with public-sector digital initiatives.

Digital is seen as a way to improve public service delivery — increasing efficiency, reducing cost of operations, and improving citizen satisfaction. However:

- 85% of public-sector digital projects are considered total or partial failures
- Only 55% of projects go beyond a pilot phase
- 25% of both time and cost overruns occur in projects
(Source: https://www.egov4dev.org/success/sfrates.shtml)

Challenges causing these failures:
1. High risk of Tech projects — Stakeholders leading projects have high churn; limited funding vs. high IT modernization costs — leading to Stalled or Dropped Projects
2. Lack of Internal Agency — Dependency on IT vendors creating bottlenecks; lack of in-house staff in cloud, DevOps, or digital services (60% of agencies) — leading to Limited Agility and Slow Iteration
3. Multiple Iterations to get it right — Requirements lost in translation between policy teams and technical implementers — leading to Fragmented citizen experience

A new approach is needed:
Traditional Approach problems: IT departments control development (heavy vendor dependency), lengthy approval and development cycles (months to implement changes), high vendor dependency (costly customization), fragmented citizen experiences.

Modern Approach (DIGIT Studio): Program managers configure services directly (no coding), rapid service delivery in weeks instead of months, agile adaptation to policy changes, user-centred responsive and accessible digital services.

"By 2026, 80% of new applications will be built by non-IT users" — Gartner

DIGIT Studio Capabilities: What you can build and do.

Create:
- Forms (drag-drop form builder for citizen service requests)
- Workflows (define states, actions, and role assignments)
- Notifications
- Roles
- Business Rules
- Dashboards and Reports

Validate:
- Demo to Stakeholders
- Create A/B versions to test with users
- No time lag between development and preview
- System checks for common errors and practices

Iterate:
- Make changes to pre-created apps
- Rollback to previous versions

Launch:
- Upload and Manage Data
- Create Users
- Deploy with 1 click
- Monitor Performance and Adoption
- Scale apps across regions

Share and Reuse:
- Access apps created by the Ecosystem/within instance
- Publish apps for others to reuse
- Replicate Best Practices

DIGIT Studio creates value for every stakeholder.

Government Department Leaders:
- Reduce risk and cost of digitization
- Improve citizen satisfaction
- Scale across regions
- Deliver visible impact within their tenure

Program Managers:
- Stay responsive to changing needs
- Align stakeholders with ease
- Deliver visible impact faster

Implementation Partners:
- Stay aligned with program managers
- Cut back-and-forth delays
- Deliver compliant services faster

User Journey with DIGIT Studio — How to build and deploy a service step by step.

Step 1 — Login & Onboarding: Secure login and guided tour of platform features.
Step 2 — Create Service Form: Use drag-drop form builder for citizen service requests.
Step 3 — Configure Workflow: Define states, actions, and role assignments.
Step 4 — Add Documents & Notifications: Attach requirements, checklists, and alerts.
Step 5 — Preview & Test: Simulate user experience with sample data.
Step 6 — Publish & Deploy: One-click deployment to production.

To create a new service/workflow in DIGIT Studio:
1. Log in and click 'Create a New Service' from the App Dashboard
2. Build your service form using the drag-drop builder
3. Configure the workflow — define states (e.g. SUBMITTED, IN_REVIEW, APPROVED) and actions (e.g. SUBMIT, APPROVE, REJECT) with role assignments
4. Add documents and notifications for each workflow state
5. Preview and test the full flow with sample data
6. Publish and deploy with one click

What DIGIT Studio delivers — Key metrics and benefits.

- Less than 1 Week to Build an App on Studio: Launch new services in days instead of months
- 50-70% Time Saving: Minimize back-and-forth communication delay
- Reusable Templates with Built-in Best Practices: Pre-configured templates embed proven public service delivery practices, helping teams design services faster and more effectively
- Powered by DIGIT: Leverages the DIGIT platform's secure, scalable foundation to deliver citizen services with confidence and long-term reliability

How is DIGIT Studio different from other Low Code No Code apps?

The market trend: By 2026, 80% of new applications will be built by non-IT users. Enterprise platforms (Power Platform, AppSheet, Oracle APEX, SAP, Palantir) enable drag-and-drop app creation.

The gap with existing tools:
- Enterprise-first, not purpose-built for public services
- High licensing costs and complex IT setup
- Lack of pre-built government modules
- Difficult to adapt to policy and governance needs

DIGIT Studio Differentiation:
- Purpose-built for government: Pre-configured components for public services
- End-to-end service lifecycle: Apply → Verify → Notify → Deliver
- Ready-to-use government modules: Citizen Portal, Employee Portal, Document Configurator, Notification Manager etc.

How service delivery changes with DIGIT Studio — Before vs After.

Before DIGIT Studio:
- IT/vendor dependency: Technical gatekeepers for every change
- Weeks of back-and-forth: Even for small service changes
- Fragmented citizen experiences: Inconsistent interfaces across services
- Delays and overruns: 80% of projects exceed timelines

With DIGIT Studio:
- Business users configure directly: No coding skills required
- Rapid iteration in days: Test and deploy changes quickly
- Unified citizen experience: Consistent interface across services
- Faster rollout, visible impact: Services live in weeks, not months

The DIGIT Studio Impact:
- Faster services
- More responsive government
- Better citizen outcomes

Ready to transform service delivery?
- Start with a pilot service in weeks, not months
- Empower your teams to configure without coding
- Scale successful services across departments

DIGIT Studio connects governments directly to citizens — no-code tools bridge the digital divide.
